name: Kailash Satyarthi
old: Shri Kailash Satyarthi — born Kailash Sharma on 11 January 1954 in Vidisha, Madhya Pradesh — is India's first natural-born Nobel Peace Laureate, sharing the 2014 prize with Malala Yousafzai. An engineer who walked away from the drawing board to walk into bonded-labour quarries, he founded Bachpan Bachao Andolan (1980), GoodWeave (1994), the Global March Against Child Labour (1998) and, most recently, the Satyarthi Movement for Global Compassion (2024). More than 138,000 children stand freed in his wake. He speaks softly, marches long, and calls compassion the oxygen of humanity.

name: Mohan Munasinghe
old: Born in Colombo in July 1945 to an engineer-father and a literary mother, Professor Mohan Munasinghe climbed Cambridge, MIT, McGill and Concordia before returning to counsel presidents and the World Bank. As Vice-Chair of the IPCC's Fourth Assessment, he shared the 2007 Nobel Peace Prize with the IPCC and Al Gore; the 2021 Blue Planet Prize followed for his Sustainomics framework. Officer of the Legion of Honour, Deshamanya of Sri Lanka, founder of the MIND institute in Colombo, he champions peace as inseparable from sustainable development — convening climate, equity and dignity under one patient sky.

name: Ramdas Athawale
old: Hon'ble Shri Ramdas Bandu Athawale — born 25 December 1959 in Agalgaon, Sangli — is Dalit Maharashtra's most visible political couplet-maker. Founder-President of the Republican Party of India (Athawale) since 1999, Rajya Sabha MP since 2014 (re-elected unopposed 2020), and continuously Union Minister of State for Social Justice and Empowerment since 2016 through Modi 3.0, he is Marathi poet, Ambedkarite, Buddhist and film actor rolled into one. His rhyming Hindi verses ("Modi hai toh mumkin hai"; "Go Corona Go") sit beside a serious record on reservations and Scheduled-Caste welfare — a politician who legislates in prose and campaigns in rhyme. डू

name: Tehemton Burjor Mirza
old: From a Dadar classroom to the sanctum of Iranshah, Vada Dasturji Tehemton Burjor Mirza walked the old athornan road in a modern suit. He sold and strategised in Mumbai's market-rooms, yet his lineage — priests on mother's and father's side — kept calling toward Udvada, where a thousand-year-old flame has never yielded to night. name: Pradeep Gandhi
old: News on 12 December 2005), in which Shri Gandhi was among eleven Members of Parliament (ten Lok Sabha, one Rajya Sabha) filmed accepting cash (reportedly ₹55,000) in exchange for undertaking to table fictitious parliamentary questions. A Special Committee of the Lok Sabha held him guilty of contempt of the House; the House adopted the motion for his expulsion. The matter is commonly referenced in Indian parliamentary history as the "Cash-for-Query scandal". Source: Wikipedia (Pradeep Gandhi); Wikipedia (14th Lok Sabha); en-academic.com. Twenty years on, no criminal conviction is on public record; the sanction was parliamentary (expulsion), not criminal. BFP Bureau should consider press-handling sensitivity and confirm current party-political status before any public-facing programme reference Shri Pradeep Gandhi — born 26 December 1964; BJP politician from Rajnandgaon, Chhattisgarh — rose through Zila Parishad chairmanship to the State Assembly (2004) and the 14th Lok Sabha before being expelled from Parliament on 23 December 2005 in the Cobrapost "Operation Duryodhana" Cash-for-Query sting that caught him and ten other MPs on camera accepting cash to table fictitious questions. Two decades on, he runs The Prithvi Foundation as National President and serves as National Secretary of the All India Ex-MPs Association. The expansion of "UFME" in his BFP designation could not be independently corroborated. A public life rebuilt in a parliamentary shadow that will not fade.

name: Bhupesh Kumar
old: About the Foundation: Non-profit established in memory of Mr. Aryaman Verma — Ludhiana-born Sat Paul Mittal School alumnus (Class X 94.8%, Class XII 82%), India Book of Records "youngest technocrat" for the Shudhvayu air purifier, country's youngest drone developer, ISRO YUVIKA Young Scientist Program selectee, 1st Prize Project Competition on Space Technology at Lovely Professional University, 1st Runner-up at South Asia's largest robotics competition (Moscow). name: Bhupesh Kumar
old:
Republic Day 2026 — District Administration Award
On 26 January 2026, the District Administration of Ludhiana formally honoured Aryaman Foundation (Regd.) at the Republic Day function for two strands of work — "helping families battling cancer and promoting robotics education among children". The Foundation team describes the moment as "a deeply emotional and proud moment … what began as a tribute in Aryaman's memory is growing into a movement of service and responsibility."
Co-launched the Ludhiana Young Achievers Award (7 February 2025)
The Foundation jointly launched the "Ludhiana Young Achievers Award" at DCM Young Entrepreneur School. The launch was led under the able guidance of Dr. Anirudh Gupta (CEO, DCM YES) and was endorsed at-presence by Mr. Jitender Jorwal, IAS (Deputy Commissioner of Ludhiana), Mr. D.R. Bhatti, IPS (Retd.) (former DGP), Mr. Kuldip Bawa, PCS, and Mr. Prince Johar (Deputy Mayor, Ludhiana). On 21 March 2025 the inaugural ceremony facilitated 25 young achievers, each receiving certificates and gifts of appreciation at DCM YES School.
Government of India recognition — NGO Darpan (21 August 2024)
The Aryaman Foundation was registered with NGO Darpan, NITI Aayog, Government of India under registration PB/2024/0442848 — making it eligible to receive grants from Central Ministries and to file Requests-for-Proposals with major corporates and CSR funds. The Foundation is also registered under the Societies Registration Act with the Punjab state government (8 August 2024).
Health-system partnership recognition (5 June 2025)
The Foundation team met Dr. Balbir Singh, Hon'ble Minister of Health, Government of Punjab to discuss enrolling needy families in the Ayushman Bharat health-coverage scheme for cancer treatment support — a working-level recognition of the Foundation's role in last-mile patient access. On 10 August 2025, with Dr. Balbir Singh and the Philanthropy Club, the Foundation participated in the inauguration of the new Thalassemia Ward at Civil Hospital, Ludhiana, donating toys to paediatric thalassemia patients.
Notable peer-recognised initiatives (not formal awards but cited publicly):

SMARTH — paediatric cancer support programme; supported children including Pratik (DMCH), Sehar (3 yrs, CMC), Rupa (1 yr 8 m, kidney tumour, CMC), Rohandeep (19 yrs, blood cancer), Khushdeep Singh (14 yrs, CMC), Jasveer Kaur (14 yrs, bone cancer, DMC), Navraj Kaur (3 months, CMC), Charu Mishra (oral cancer), Veena Sharma (SPS Apollo), Deepak Sharma's family.
WHIZAARU — robotics & AI education programme; held WHIZFEST 2.0, ran workshops at Government High School Sarabha Nagar, Government Girls School Bhawani Garh, Indus World School (Metaverse / MetaQuest demos), and inaugurated a Robotics-and-Computer Lab at Bal Bhawan on Diwali Eve (20 October 2025).
HOPE — cancer-patient financial-emotional support.
National Space Day Quiz (23 August 2024) — 250 students across Ludhiana, Kolkata, Delhi, Rajasthan, Jaipur, Pune, Varanasi, Nagpur, Udaipur, Meerut, Kanpur, Guwahati, Bangalore, Jalandhar, Chandigarh.
Blood Donation Camp (15 July 2025) — 110 units collected in association with Lodhi Club and the Red Foundation, with Civil Hospital & DMCH support teams.
Walkathon 2025 — flagged off 12 January 2025.

No personal awards for Mr. Bhupesh Kumar that I could find on the Foundation's website, blog, or in public records. All institutional recognition flows to the Foundation collectively, with Mrs. Dimple Verma (President) named publicly as the lead spokesperson. The Republic Day 2026 award accepted on the Foundation's behalf is the most significant formal honour to date, and Mr. Bhupesh Kumar — as the Foundation's General Secretary — would be one of the office-bearers in whom that recognition vests.

name: Rui Duarte de Barros
old: H.E. Mr. Rui Duarte de Barros is a Bissau-Guinean economist, Havana-trained engineer and twice-serving Prime Minister of the Republic of Guinea-Bissau. A former Minister of Finance and long-serving commissioner at UEMOA, he was first called to lead a transitional cabinet in May 2012 in the tense aftermath of the April coup, shepherding the country toward the 2014 elections. Returning to the Palácio do Governo in January 2024 under President Embaló, he steered a fragile coalition until his dismissal in August 2025. Soft-spoken, technocratic and discreet, he embodies a generation of Bissau-Guinean public servants devoted to the hard disciplines of stability.

Born in 1962 in Dudhala, a village in Amreli, he walked into Surat as a boy of thirteen with barely twelve rupees, and built an organisation now known equally for its "Loyalty Bonus" programme of cars, homes and jewellery, and for the Dholakia Foundation's water-conservation and tree-planting work across Gujarat. The Government of India conferred the Padma Shri on him in 2022 for social work.

"not fit and proper" to act as a commodity derivatives broker, citing reputational erosion linked to the 2013 NSEL payment-default scam (order contested). SEBI (May 2022) imposed a ₹25 lakh penalty on MOFSL for misutilisation of client funds and incorrect margin reporting. SEBI Adjudication Order (December 2024, ref. 90409) imposed a ₹7 lakh penalty on MOFSL for margin-reporting lapses, 334 unresolved SCORES / exchange complaints, bank-book lapses and an improper ₹3.5 crore settlement affecting 39 inactive clients. SEBI Adjudication Order (June 2025) imposed a ₹3 lakh penalty on MOFSL for weak control over authorised-persons' trading terminals and fund-based activities. Cybersecurity (February 2024) — MOFSL disclosed a ransomware incident (LockBit); threat actor claimed data of more than 6 million clients was affected. EOW Mumbai / SFIO — supplementary charge-sheet naming MOFSL alongside IIFL and Anand Rathi for alleged mis- selling of NSEL paired contracts, KYC manipulation and client-code modification; no personal criminal conviction of Mr. Motilal Oswal reported CA Motilal Gopilal Oswal is the co-founder, Chairman and Managing Director of Motilal Oswal Financial Services Limited — the Mumbai-headquartered financial-services house he built with Shri Raamdeo Agrawal from a single BSE sub-broker's badge issued in 1987. Born in 1962 in the Rajasthan border village of Padru, he trained as a Chartered Accountant in Mumbai and has since shepherded MOFSL (BSE 532892; NSE MOTILALOFS) across broking, asset management, wealth management, private equity and home finance. The Motilal Oswal Foundation carries his conviction that wealth, once learned and earned, is made whole only when returned.

name: Margarita Louis-Dreyfus
old: Born 1 July 1962 in Leningrad to an electrical engineer's granddaughter, Ms. Margarita Louis-Dreyfus has become one of the quiet sovereigns of global agriculture. Law-trained in Moscow, economics-trained in Leningrad, she met Robert Louis-Dreyfus on a 1988 flight and married him in 1992; on his death in 2009, she took the chair of LDC — one of the world's "ABCD" commodity houses founded in 1851. Through her Akira family trust she holds 96.2% of the Group; in 2016 she sold Olympique de Marseille, in 2020 she welcomed Abu Dhabi's ADQ as partner. Zurich-based, mother of five, she arrives at the Mumbai Conclave as a steward of continuity — wheat, rice, sugar, coffee, cotton, rice and dairy all moving quietly beneath her gaze.

name: Ravindra Sadanand Phatak
old: Shri Ravindra Phatak, his wife Smt Varsha Phatak, and seven associates for allegedly cheating businessman Shri Rajendra Saraogi of approximately ₹26 crore through criminal breach of trust and cheating — under IPC Sections 406, 420, 120B and other provisions. The complaint alleged that Phatak and his group had induced Saraogi to invest in a Thane construction project, promising returns and property allotments that were not delivered. The case proceeded through a decade of legal proceedings in the Metropolitan Magistrate's court at Girgaon, Mumbai. Phatak's defence, led by lawyer Shri Prerak Choudhary, contended that the matter constituted a civil contractual disagreement rather than a criminal offence, lacking the requisite mens rea. Phatak and all co-accused (including wife and seven associates) were acquitted by the Mumbai court in August 2025 after the decade-long trial (per Grokipedia, citing Indian court records). Separate unrelated matter: A Sena MLC was booked in 2018 for distributing cash in Maharashtra's Palghar (reported in press; institutional context, connection to this subject to be confirmed by BFP Bureau). Recommended press-handling: matter is now resolved (acquittal August 2025) but remains a disclosure-worthy pre-BFP record — BFP Bureau should be aware before any public-programme hosting reference. No sub judice status remains वीं द्र Shri Ravindra Sadanand Phatak — born circa 1966 in Thane district, Maharashtra; construction-business owner and Director of Saishradha Infracon (India) Private Limited — served as Member of the Legislative Council (MLC) for Thane Local Authorities constituency from June 2016 to May 2022, having defeated five-time MLC Shri Vasant Davkhare of the NCP by 151 votes on 6 June 2016. A former Thane Municipal Corporation Standing Committee Chairman (2005 and 2012) and corporator (2002, 2007, 2012, 2015), he defected from Congress to Shiv Sena in July 2014. On 23 June 2022, he famously joined the Eknath Shinde rebel camp in Guwahati — two days after Uddhav Thackeray had sent him to Surat to negotiate with those very MLAs. The register's "MLC" designation requires correction: his MLC term ended in May 2022 and he is not currently a sitting member of the Maharashtra Vidhan Parishad. In August 2025 he and his wife Smt Varsha Phatak along with seven associates were acquitted after a decade-long trial in the Azad Maidan 2015 FIR alleging ₹26 crore in cheating / criminal breach of trust against complainant Shri Rajendra Saraogi; the defence successfully argued the matter was a civil contractual dispute rather than a criminal offence. No criminal conviction remains on public record; matter is not sub judice.

name: Salim Merchant
old: Mr Salim (Sadruddin Moledina) Merchant — born 3 March 1974 Mumbai; St. Peter's High School and KC College alumnus; Trinity College of Music London (piano) and Bharatiya Vidya Bhavan Delhi (harmonium)-trained under Ustad Sultan Khan — is Champion-category BFP attendee (code 1AL.23, Artist & Literature track) and one half of the celebrated Salim-Sulaiman composer duo (with brother Sulaiman Merchant, b. 21 December 1972). Over 100+ Bollywood film scores to his credit — including Bhoot (2003 breakthrough), Dhoom (2004), Krrish (2006), Chak De! India (2007; its title track became an unofficial national sports-and-patriotism anthem), Rab Ne Bana Di Jodi (2008), Fashion (2008), Kurbaan (2009), and 2014's "Ishq Wala Love" from Student of the Year. Performed live at the 2010 FIFA World Cup opening ceremony and co-wrote the anthem "Africa – You're a Star." Nominated for a 2009 Daytime Emmy Award for "We Like Being Small" from Nickelodeon's Wonder Pets!; remixed Lady Gaga's "Born This Way" and "Judas" and Enrique Iglesias's "I'm A Freak." Judge on Indian Idol Seasons 5 and 6, Indian Idol Junior Season 2, and The Voice of India Season 2. SOCAN member. Runs Merchant Records and the annual Bhoomi folk-music compilation project. Spouse Jeanne Micheal Merchant runs a Mumbai toddler-music academy since 2013; daughter Ayesha. Ancestral Bohra/Ismaili roots from Mundra, Kutch; original surname Moledina. One 2019 plagiarism allegation (Farhan Saeed re "Haareya" vs "Roiyaan") — Salim called it a coincidence; no legal finding. Attendance subject to Salim Sulaiman Live tour conflict-check per 10 April 2026 confirmation.

Born September 1975, he holds eleven active UK directorships — including Crown Top Properties Ltd (from 1999), ASL & Co Scotland Ltd and ASL & Co LLP (from 2013), LSCIH and BTIEI (development capital), and the six-company Lal Sharma group incorporated in May 2024 — together representing a Scottish property, infrastructure and venture-capital footprint with combined active- company net worth of c. £2m. name: Achyuta Samanta
old: Professor Achyuta Samanta (born 20 January 1965, Kalarabanka, Odisha) is the founder of the Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology (1992) and the Kalinga Institute of Social Sciences (1993), twin Bhubaneswar institutions that today educate some ninety thousand students, among them fifty thousand tribal children taught, fed and housed entirely free. An M.Sc. in chemistry orphaned at four, he raised a university on five thousand rupees and a mother's faith. A former Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha MP, he holds more than seventy honorary doctorates and civilian honours from Bahrain and Mongolia.

name: Leymah Gbowee
old: Ms Leymah Roberta Gbowee is the Liberian peace activist and social worker whose white-clad sit-ins at the Monrovia fish market helped end a fourteen-year civil war. A 2011 Nobel Peace Laureate — honoured with Ellen Johnson Sirleaf and Tawakkol Karman "for their non-violent struggle for the safety of women and for women's rights to full participation in peace-building work" — she founded the Gbowee Peace Foundation Africa in Monrovia in February 2012 and now also directs CUNY's Institute on Gender, Law, and Transformative Peace. Trained trauma counsellor, memoirist of Mighty Be Our Powers, mother of six: an ordinary woman who refused to let the bullets have the last word.
